title: MatrixEvent
description: Collection of all possible actions for the room core

AnswerCallContent.description: Event used when answering an invite event

duration.description: Duration of the audio clip in ms

size.description: Size of the audio file in bytes

CallCandidatesContent.description: Event used to exchange viable ICE candidates with the other party upon answering a call

CallInviteContent.description: Initial event to invite other parties to a call

invitee.description: DID of the called user. Any user in room may answer if omitted

lifetime.description: Time in ms during which invite is valid after sending this event

CallNegotiationContent.description: Event used to renegotiate between participants. First an offer containing a lifetime is sent. Other participants then send an answer. Offer and answer should never both be set. To ensure this they are not public to force the users to use the setters.

answer.description: Session description object for negotioation answers

lifetime.description: Time in ms before offer timeout

offer.description: Session description object for negotioation offers

EventContent.description:
Simple enum to fit the different possible contents. Unique event type string can be generated from this using pattern matching.

# State events

All events that in some way alter the state of a room

- Room name - Room topic - Room avatar - Pinned events in room

# Call Events

All call events share the call_id, party_id and version fields call_id: A unique ID that that are used to determine which call events correspond to each other party_id: A unique ID to identify a call participant. May but not must be used for multiple calls. Must be unique across all participants. version: The version of the VoIP specs used for the message. This version is "1". A string is used for experimental versions.

# Ephemeral events

Ephemeral events are once-off events that do not need to be saved.

# User events

Events for interacting with users profiles

FileInfo.description: Contains metadata of any other filetypes

size.description: Size of the file in bytes

thumbnail_file.description: CID to an image file that is to be used as the thumbnail

HangupCallContent.description: Hangup event used to signal the termination of the call.

HangupCallReason.description: Enum containg possible reasons for a hangup event

oneOf.description: ICE negotiation has failed and connection could not be established
Connection failed after some media was exchanged. Includes when renegotiation fails if media was sent prviously
The other party did not answer in time
User actively chooses to end the call
Client was unable to start capturing media in such a way that it is unable to continue the call
User is busy. Exists primarily for bridging and does not include when user is in a call already
Some other error occured that is not described by the above

ICECandidate.description: ICE candidate for WebRTC exchange protocol

size.description: Size of the image file in bytes

thumbnail_file.description: CID to an image file that is to be used as the thumbnail

LocationInfo.description: Contains metadata of any location based content

thumbnail_file.description: CID to an image file that is to be used as the thumbnail

Mentions.description: Used to describe which users got mentioned in the body of a message

MessageType.description: Events that sent actual messages that can be seen by all participants in a room.

oneOf.description: Formatted body and format are not pub to ensure with setters that formatted body is only set when a format is also given.

format.description: The format used in formatted body

formatted_body.description: A formatted version of the body

mentions.description: Users that are mentioned in the body

oneOf.description: Formatted body and format are not pub to ensure with setters that formatted body is only set when a format is also given

format.description: The format used in formatted body

formatted_body.description: A formatted version of the body

mentions.description: Users that are mentioned in the body

body.description: A text representing the image in some way
A text representing the audio in same way
A text representing the file in some way

geo_uri.description: A geo uri by definition of https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5870

oneOf.description: All events that interact with or create a poll

body.description: A textual representation of the poll, i.e. the question

answers.description: List of IDs of the answers the user has responded with

oneOf.description: Event that closes the poll. For undisclosed and anonymous polls, this is the point where the reults are shown.

body.description: Textual representation of the poll ending

PollAnswer.description: One possible answer in a poll. ID should be unique across answers.

id.description: Unique ID to identify an answer

kind.description: What kind of poll this is

max_selections.description: The maximum number of answers users can select. Default is 1 and cannot be less

question.description: The question the poll was created for

oneOf.description: In disclosed polls all participants can see the already cast votes (including who cast them)
In undisclosed polls the votes will only appear when the poll has ended
As undisclosed but voters will stay hidden even after poll has ended

content.description: Content ID for a json file containing the story data

display_time.description: How long the story will be shown once opened in ms

lifetime.description: How long users can view the story after it was posted in ms

PresenceContent.description: Event content that is used to inform other users of the presence status. In contrast to typing events, the sender is important here and always corresponds to the user the information is about.

avatar.description: Avatar the user is currently using

currently_active.description: Whether the user is currently active

last_active.description: Timestampt in milliseconds when the user last performed an action

status_msg.description: An optional arbitrary description to accompany the presence

PresenceType.description:
Basic enum for possible presence states of a specific user

Online: Default state when user is connected to an event stream

Offline: The user is not connected to an event stream or is actively suppressing this information

DnD: As 'Online' but the user doesn't want to be disturbed

PublicReceiptContent.description: These receipts are always sent into a room and indicate to all users that the messages sent up to the indicated event were read by the user that sent this receipt event. This becomes public knowledge to all users participating in the CO.

m_read.description: The ID of the latest event read by the user

thread_id.description: The ID of the thread if receipt is threaded

ReactionContent.description: Empty content as the only purpose is holding a relation to another event. Mostly used for annotation events

RedactionContent.description: Event content used to redact other events. Sender of this event must be either the same as the sender of the original event or a user with the necessary permissions. Redactions are idempotent and irreversible. They do not use the same relation fields as other events

reason.description: An optional reason field mostly used when event got redacted by another user

redacts.description: Event ID of the redacted event

RejectCallContent.description: Event sent if call was rejected by a user.

RelationType.description: Simple enum containing all different types of relation that events can have to other events

SelectCallAnswerContent.description: Event used to select one of possibly multiple call answers

selected_party_id.description: Party id of the participant whose answer has been selected

SessionDescription.description: Session description object for sdp offers and answers

ThumbnailInfo.description: Contains metadata of images used as a thumbnail

size.description: Size of the image file in bytes

TypingContent.description: Event used to indicate which users in the room are currently typing. Gets sent to all active users. For direct messages this information will only be shared with the other participant. Information should be updated regularly and have a timout after which no users should count as typing when no new event was sent.

user_ids.description: List of users currently typing in the room

avatar.description: Content ID pointing to the avatar of the user

display_name.description: The name that the user likes to use as a default

status_msg.description: The current status of the user

duration.description: Duration of the video clip in ms

size.description: Size of the image file in bytes

thumbnail_file.description: CID to an image file that is to be used as the thumbnail

story.description: ID of the event that containes the viewed story

relates_to.description: Used in some event contents to define a relation to other events

event_id.description: The ID of the event that is being related to

in_reply_to.description: Special relation to depict replies. Listed extra as this can happen with the other relations simultaneously

key.description: Used for annotations. Defines the type of emoji that has been reacted with.

room_id.description: The ID of the room the related-to event is in. Only needed for forwarding.
